text: That which is below corresponds to that which is above, and that which is above corresponds to that which is below, to accomplish the miracle of the One Thing.
context_full: The Emerald Tablet is the most condensed and influential text in Western esoteric tradition — its central axiom encodes the Hermetic law of correspondence. Every level of reality mirrors every other: the cosmos reflects the soul, the soul reflects the body, the body reflects the stars. This is not metaphor but a structural claim — that reality is self-similar across scales, each part encoding the whole. Modern systems theory and fractal mathematics arrived at similar conclusions through secular means. For the Hermetic practitioner, it is an invitation to read the visible world as a map of the invisible, and vice versa.
reflection_prompt: Name one outer pattern in your life — a recurring conflict, a habit in your relationships, a stuck situation. Where do you find its exact counterpart inside yourself? What changes when you take ownership of that inner correspondent?

text: As above, so below; as below, so above; as within, so without; as without, so within.
context_full: The full formula of the Hermetic law of correspondence, extending the Emerald Tablet's principle in all four directions: vertical (above/below) and horizontal (within/without). The cosmos and the psyche are not separate domains but two aspects of the same reality — what the mystic finds in the depths of meditation, the astronomer finds written in the structure of galaxies. This does not collapse the distinction between inner and outer; it reveals them as two languages for the same truth.
reflection_prompt: Spend five minutes today observing your inner weather — your mood, your quality of attention, the tension or ease in your body. Then look at your outer circumstances with the same attention. What correspondences do you notice?

text: Mind is the Good, and if it be so, then Mind is God.
context_full: In Hermetic cosmology, Mind (Nous) is the first emanation of the divine — the creative intelligence through which all things come into being. To identify Mind with the Good is to collapse the apparent distinction between knowing rightly and living rightly. Intellectual and moral cultivation are the same activity. This stands against the modern tendency to treat thinking as a neutral tool that can be aimed at any end. For Hermes, the quality of your thinking is inseparable from the quality of your soul.
reflection_prompt: Consider what you give your mind to today — what problems, images, and mental loops occupy it most. Are these leading you toward what is Good? What would it look like to orient your mental life deliberately toward your highest values?

text: If then thou dost not make thyself equal to God, thou canst not apprehend God; for like is known by like.
context_full: This passage states a principle found across ancient philosophy: that knowledge requires a certain likeness between knower and known. The eye can see light because light is already in it; the soul can know God because the divine is already within it. This is not arrogance but an epistemological claim — that the path to the highest truth is not outward research but inward development. To 'make yourself equal' means to purify and enlarge your consciousness until it can hold what it seeks to understand.
reflection_prompt: Is there a quality — patience, courage, compassion, clarity — that you admire deeply in others but struggle to find in yourself? Consider: your recognition of it is evidence that some version of it already lives in you. What would it mean to develop it?

text: Leap clear of all that is corporeal, and make thyself grow to a like expanse with that greatness which is beyond all measure; rise above all time and become Eternal.
context_full: This passage is an exercise in mental expansion, one of the core practices of Hermetic philosophy. Hermes instructs the student to imaginatively transcend every limit — body, time, place, scale — until the mind touches something unlimited. This is not escapism but a method: by practicing boundlessness in imagination, the practitioner discovers something in themselves that is genuinely not bounded by death or circumstance. The Hermetic path is one of ever-expanding identification with what is largest in existence.
reflection_prompt: Close your eyes and spend two minutes imaginatively expanding — let your awareness grow beyond your body, your city, your era, your lifespan. What remains when you have moved past all limits? What does that feel like?

text: Think not that God is invisible. No one is more manifest than God. He made all things for this reason alone — that through them you might see Him.
context_full: This passage overturns the assumption that the divine is hidden or remote. For Hermes, the problem is not that God is absent but that the mind is absent — too occupied with its own constructions to notice the presence that saturates all things. Every created thing is not a veil over the divine but a window into it. The Hermetic path is not about getting past nature to find God; it is about learning to see nature clearly enough that the divine within it becomes visible.
reflection_prompt: Choose one ordinary thing today — a plant, a sound, a person — and give it your full, unhurried attention for several minutes. What do you find when you look past the category and into the thing itself?

text: Nothing that exists is destroyed, but men being in error call the changes which take place destruction and death.
context_full: Hermetic cosmology holds that the universe is eternal — not the particular forms within it, but the substance of which they are made. What we call death is transformation: the reorganization of what was never created from nothing and can never become nothing. This teaching runs through Stoicism, Neoplatonism, and modern physics alike. Its practical value is not consolation but perspective: the losses that grieve us are real as changes, but not real as destructions. Something always continues.
reflection_prompt: Bring to mind something you have lost — a relationship, a version of yourself, an opportunity. Can you see it as transformed rather than destroyed? What, if anything, persists or continues in a changed form?

text: Silence is the great mystery. Silence is the teacher of those who seek the truth.
God sent Mind unto men as a gift. But not all men have received it; some have their share of it, some have not.
Think that nothing is impossible for thee; think thyself immortal and able to understand all arts, all sciences, the way of every life.
Knowledge is the end of impiety, and the beginning of the knowing of God.
The Father of all is Mind; Life and Light. He gave birth to Man, a Being like unto Himself, whom He loved as His own child.
Its father is the Sun; its mother the Moon. The wind carries it in its belly; the earth is its nurse.

Separate the earth from the fire, the subtle from the gross, acting with prudent judgment. It ascends from the earth to the heaven, and again descends to the earth, and receives the power of the superior and inferior things.
Thus will you have the glory of the whole world, and all darkness will fly from you.
It is the source of all perfection throughout the universe.
Nothing is swifter than Mind; nothing broader; nothing more pleasant.
The soul that has seen the Good longs to return thither; and it does return, for such is the Good.
The road is hard to travel in the beginning, when you first set out; it is unfamiliar and quite impassable, and full of gloom. But when you have traveled it, you may travel it well enough.
The Word came forth from the Light, and it is the son of God.
Truth is not hidden; it is men who are hidden from truth by the veil of their passions.
The universe is not a collection of dead matter but a living being, full of mind and soul.
What is within us must agree with what is above us, and what is above us agrees with what is within us.
Seek not the things of earth, for they perish; seek the things of heaven, for they are eternal and never change.
Man is a great wonder, a living being worthy of worship and reverence, for he passes into the nature of a god as though he were himself a god.
There is one Mind which is the maker of all, and it makes all things by thought alone.
All is one, and one is all — in God, for God contains all.
He who has understood the way of Fate shall have no Master.
The wise man rules the stars; the fool obeys them.
Death is not the end of things but their transformation.
To know God, you must be like God. Make yourself grow — ascend beyond time, become eternity.
God is not invisible; He is the most visible of all things. He is so visible that all things become visible through Him.
